text.title: Viral Loop Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Viral Loop Optimization` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Optimize c (invitee conversion) BEFORE i (invites per user). c improvements multiply every subsequent loop turn — i improvements only add invites.

rationale.source: Brian Balfour viral compounding math; Reforge curriculum

rule.rationale: c is the multiplier; each lift in c amplifies all future loop turns. i is additive — improves one cycle. c-first sequencing produces compound returns.

rule.statement: Each A/B test MUST change ONE variable at a time (CTA copy, OR share affordance, OR incentive amount). Multivariate viral tests confound which lever moved K.
rule.rationale: Multivariate viral tests rarely have sample size for clean attribution. One-variable tests are slower but legible — team learns the loop, not just hits a number.

rule.statement: Decisions to ship MUST use K_eff (K * D30 retention), not raw K. Lifts in raw K with no K_eff move are not real wins.
rule.rationale: Raw K can rise because invitees signup but never activate. K_eff catches this; raw K alone leads to shipping cosmetic lifts.

rule.statement: Share moments MUST be designed AT a user win (achievement, output, completed action), not interruptively. Interruptive prompts depress retention more than they grow i.
rule.rationale: Interruptive ('Invite 3 friends to continue!') triggers quit-impulse. Win-anchored ('Share your report — it looks great') feels native + ships authentically.

rule.statement: If a variant shows no statistically significant lift after 2 full viral cycle times, kill it. Long-tail belief that 'it'll work eventually' is the #1 source of waste.
rule.rationale: Loop experiments have stable signal by 2 cycle times. Beyond that = no effect or too small. Killing fast frees capacity for the next lever.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
